summary: arch_tool_secrets_layer misses canonical Redacted<T> isolation pattern from database_security_doctrine.v1.json
details: The precedent subagent confirmed that the plan ignores the established Redacted<T> credential isolation pattern defined in database_security_doctrine.v1.json. This creates a 2-layer architecture (prevention + detection) when the codebase's security philosophy is 3-layer (isolation + prevention + detection). The plan also repeats convergence gaps the RL-004 critic explicitly flagged: no reference to database_security_doctrine, no composition with Redacted types, and treats the subprocess-spawn audit as novel when Phase 2.0 of DB security doctrine already plans it. The SecretScanner should be in packages/core, not packages/opencode.

summary: ENV_BLOCKLIST is duplicated between packages/core and packages/desktop — divergent evolution risk
details: The coroner subagent discovered that ENV_BLOCKLIST exists as an identical copy in both packages/core/src/util/env-blocklist.ts and packages/desktop/src/main/env-blocklist.ts. The arch_tool_secrets_layer plan adds entries to only one copy (packages/core). If the desktop copy isn't updated, tool subprocesses and desktop sidecar will have diverging env protection. No test verifies the two copies remain synchronized.

summary: Plan's fix grouping is incorrect — fix-A and fix-G share smart-bash.ts but are in different rollback groups
details: The appeal subagent proved that fix-A (sanitizedProcessEnv in smart-bash.ts) and fix-G (scanToolOutput in smart-bash.ts) modify the same file but are placed in different groups (Group B: Prevention, Group C: Detection). Reverting Group B while keeping Group C creates a half-state: bash has secret detection but no env prevention. The scanner finds secrets that could have been blocked. This is the wrong grouping for independent reversibility. Correct grouping: fix-A + fix-F + fix-G should be GROUP BASH (atomic), fixes B/C/D/E should be GROUP OTHER_TOOLS (independent).

summary: project-command.ts has broken import — env: sanitizedProcessEnv() at line 74 but no import of sanitizedProcessEnv
details: Blast-radius subagent discovered that project-command.ts line 74 already has `env: sanitizedProcessEnv()` in the Bun.spawn call but line 1-2 imports are still `import path from "path"` and `import { Effect, Schema } from "effect"` — no import of sanitizedProcessEnv from @opencode-ai/core/util/opencode-process. This file is in a half-patched state that would fail typecheck. This is a pre-existing dirty file state, not part of the current plan.

summary: secret-scanner.ts already exists at packages/opencode/src/tool/secret-scanner.ts — fix-F is already implemented, only fix-G (integration) remains
details: Blast-radius subagent confirmed the file already exists with the full implementation described in the plan's fix-F. The architecture plan's apply_order should be updated: fix-F is DONE, only fix-G (smart-bash integration) needs execution. The plan's validation can skip secret-scanner unit tests (already written) and focus on integration tests.

summary: Extract inline env/scanner/analytics construction into exported functions for ≤3-line testability
details: {"verdict": "All 6 integration changes FAIL the ≤10-line testability bar. Root cause: sanitizedProcessEnv(), scanToolOutput(), and logEntry are all constructed inline inside Tool.define/Effect.gen closures with no exported accessor function. Recommendation: extract each inline value into a named exported function (e.g., getSpawnEnv(), applySecretScan(), buildBashAnalyticsEntry()) which admits ≤3-line pure-function tests without changing any behavior.", "failing_changes": ["fix-A (smart-bash env)", "fix-B (smart-grep env)", "fix-C (smart-git env)", "fix-D (smart-bun env)", "fix-E (project-command env)", "fix-G (scanner integration)", "Analytics command removal"], "passing_changes": ["fix-H (blocklist additions)", "fix-F (SecretScanner utility)"]}
